nodes.x: Wherefore, Job, I pray thee, hear my speeches, and hearken to all my words.
I said unto the Lord, Thou art my God: hear the voice of my supplications, O Lord.
I have called upon thee, for thou wilt hear me, O God: incline thine ear unto me, and hear my speech.
Hear the voice of my supplications, when I cry unto thee, when I lift up my hands toward thy holy oracle.
Hearken unto the voice of my cry, my King, and my God: for unto thee will I pray.
Now therefore, I pray thee, pardon my sin, and turn again with me, that I may worship the Lord.
Behold, now I have opened my mouth, my tongue hath spoken in my mouth.
And now I have spoken the words which the Lord God hath commanded me.
And my heart hath been secretly enticed, or my mouth hath kissed my hand:
Which my lips have uttered, and my mouth hath spoken, when I was in trouble.
Then the Lord put forth his hand, and touched my mouth. And the Lord said unto me, Behold, I have put my words in thy mouth.
I opened my mouth, and panted: for I longed for thy commandments.
My words shall be of the uprightness of my heart: and my lips shall utter knowledge clearly.
Hear; for I will speak of excellent things; and the opening of my lips shall be right things.
My mouth shall speak of wisdom; and the meditation of my heart shall be of understanding.
My lips shall greatly rejoice when I sing unto thee; and my soul, which thou hast redeemed.

And their lips shall extol the name of the Lord of Spirits,
    And righteousness before Him shall never fail,
    [And uprightness shall never fail before Him].
¶ Therefore shall ye lay up these my words in your heart and in your soul, and bind them for a sign upon your hand, that they may be as frontlets between your eyes.
The Spirit of God hath made me, and the breath of the Almighty hath given me life.
And my spirit hath rejoiced in God my Saviour.
My God hath been my support; he hath led me through mine afflictions in the wilderness; and he hath preserved me upon the waters of the great deep.
For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus hath made me free from the law of sin and death.

The Spirit of the Lord God is upon me; because the Lord hath anointed me to preach good tidings unto the meek; he hath sent me to bind up the brokenhearted, to proclaim liberty to the captives, and the opening of the prison to them that are bound;
According as his divine power hath given unto us all things that pertain unto life and godliness, through the knowledge of him that hath called us to glory and virtue:
If thou canst answer me, set thy words in order before me, stand up.
These things hast thou done, and I kept silence; thou thoughtest that I was altogether such an one as thyself: but I will reprove thee, and set them in order before thine eyes.
I cry unto thee, and thou dost not hear me: I stand up, and thou regardest me not.
Thou hast beset me behind and before, and laid thine hand upon me.
Thou renewest thy witnesses against me, and increasest thine indignation upon me; changes and war are against me.
Then call thou, and I will answer: or let me speak, and answer thou me.
Behold, I am according to thy wish in God’s stead: I also am formed out of the clay.
Remember, I beseech thee, that thou hast made me as the clay; and wilt thou bring me into dust again?
Woe unto him that striveth with his Maker! Let the potsherd strive with the potsherds of the earth. Shall the clay say to him that fashioneth it, What makest thou? or thy work, He hath no hands?
But now, O Lord, thou art our father; we are the clay, and thou our potter; and we all are the work of thy hand.

Behold, thou art Hyrum, my son ; seek the kingdom of God and all things shall be added according to that which is just. Build upon my rock, which is my gospel : deny not the spirit of revelation nor the spirit of prophecy, for wo unto him that denieth these things; therefore, treasure up in your hearts until the time which is in my wisdom that you shall go forth : behold, I speak unto all who…
But behold, I am a man, and do sin in my wish; for I ought to be content with the things which the Lord hath allotted unto me.
Behold, my terror shall not make thee afraid, neither shall my hand be heavy upon thee.
I will send my fear before thee, and will destroy all the people to whom thou shalt come, and I will make all thine enemies turn their backs unto thee.
My flesh trembleth for fear of thee; and I am afraid of thy judgments.
Be not a terror unto me: thou art my hope in the day of evil.
But as for thee and thy servants, I know that ye will not yet fear the Lord God.

This day will I begin to put the dread of thee and the fear of thee upon the nations that are under the whole heaven, who shall hear report of thee, and shall tremble, and be in anguish because of thee.
Surely thou hast spoken in mine hearing, and I have heard the voice of thy words, saying,
If now thou hast understanding, hear this: hearken to the voice of my words.
Nevertheless hear thou now this word that I speak in thine ears, and in the ears of all the people;

Yea, and thou hast also heard me when I have been cast out and have been despised by mine enemies; yea, thou didst hear my cries, and wast angry with mine enemies, and thou didst visit them in thine anger with speedy destruction.
Thou hast heard my voice: hide not thine ear at my breathing, at my cry.
I am clean without transgression, I am innocent; neither is there iniquity in me.
Verily I say unto you, ye are clean, but not all; and there is none else with whom I am well pleased;
Wash me throughly from mine iniquity, and cleanse me from my sin.
And I will cleanse them from all their iniquity, whereby they have sinned against me; and I will pardon all their iniquities, whereby they have sinned, and whereby they have transgressed against me.
But rather give alms of such things as ye have; and, behold, all things are clean unto you.
Purge ye out the iniquity which is among you; sanctify yourselves before me;
Behold, he findeth occasions against me, he counteth me for his enemy,
He hath also kindled his wrath against me, and he counteth me unto him as one of his enemies.
He that is not with me is against me: and he that gathereth not with me scattereth.
He that is not with me is against me; and he that gathereth not with me scattereth abroad.
And he that taketh not his cross, and followeth after me, is not worthy of me.
Mine enemies speak evil of me, When shall he die, and his name perish?
He putteth my feet in the stocks, he marketh all my paths.
Thou puttest my feet also in the stocks, and lookest narrowly unto all my paths; thou settest a print upon the heels of my feet.
He maketh my feet like hinds’ feet, and setteth me upon my high places.
He maketh my feet like hinds’ feet: and setteth me upon my high places.
Hold up my goings in thy paths, that my footsteps slip not.
And behold, the time cometh that he curseth your riches, that they become slippery, that ye cannot hold them; and in the days of your poverty ye cannot retain them.
Behold, in this thou art not just: I will answer thee, that God is greater than man.
Verily, verily, I say unto you, The servant is not greater than his lord; neither he that is sent greater than he that sent him.
Behold, and lo, I am the Lord thy God, and will answer thee as touching this matter.
If we receive the witness of men, the witness of God is greater: for this is the witness of God which he hath testified of his Son.
Behold, here is the man, we deliver him into thy hands; thou mayest do with him as seemeth thee good.
The Son of Man hath descended below them all. Art thou greater than he?
Why dost thou strive against him? for he giveth not account of any of his matters.
There are those who have sought occasion against him without cause;
Behold, he taketh away, who can hinder him? who will say unto him, What doest thou?
¶ Strive not with a man without cause, if he have done thee no harm.
Thou shalt not wrest the judgment of thy poor in his cause.
For God speaketh once, yea twice, yet man perceiveth it not.
God hath spoken once; twice have I heard this; that power belongeth unto God.
And if God giveth him power again, or if he translates again, or, in other words, if he bringeth forth the same words, behold, we have the same with us, and we have altered them;
And the word of the Lord came unto me the second time, saying,
But if he will not hear thee, then take with thee one or two more, that in the mouth of two or three witnesses every word may be established.
If any man speak in an unknown tongue, let it be by two, or at the most by three, and that by course; and let one interpret.
In a dream, in a vision of the night, when deep sleep falleth upon men, in slumberings upon the bed;
In thoughts from the visions of the night, when deep sleep falleth on men,
And we dreamed a dream in one night, I and he; we dreamed each man according to the interpretation of his dream.

I shall now deviate a little from my subject, in order to relate
another very singular dream which my husband had about this time, which
is as follows:--
Then he openeth the ears of men, and sealeth their instruction,
And he said unto them, He that hath ears to hear, let him hear.
He sealeth up the hand of every man; that all men may know his work.
He that hath an ear, let him hear what the Spirit saith unto the churches.
He openeth also their ear to discipline, and commandeth that they return from iniquity.
That he may withdraw man from his purpose, and hide pride from man.
The discretion of a man deferreth his anger; and it is his glory to pass over a transgression.
That they should let no pride nor haughtiness disturb their peace; that every man should esteem his neighbor as himself, laboring with their own hands for their support.
A man’s pride shall bring him low: but honour shall uphold the humble in spirit.
That he no longer should live the rest of his time in the flesh to the lusts of men, but to the will of God.
He keepeth back his soul from the pit, and his life from perishing by the sword.
Yea, his soul draweth near unto the grave, and his life to the destroyers.
He keepeth the paths of judgment, and preserveth the way of his saints.
That thou mayest give him rest from the days of adversity, until the pit be digged for the wicked.
He believeth not that he shall return out of darkness, and he is waited for of the sword.

He that abideth in this city shall die by the sword, and by the famine, and by the pestilence: but he that goeth out, and falleth to the Chaldeans that besiege you, he shall live, and his life shall be unto him for a prey.
He is chastened also with pain upon his bed, and the multitude of his bones with strong pain:
He keepeth all his bones: not one of them is broken.
His bones are full of the sin of his youth, which shall lie down with him in the dust.
So that his life abhorreth bread, and his soul dainty meat.
And when he had thus spoken, he took bread, and gave thanks to God in presence of them all: and when he had broken it, he began to eat.
This is the bread which cometh down from heaven, that a man may eat thereof, and not die.
¶ And when one of them that sat at meat with him heard these things, he said unto him, Blessed is he that shall eat bread in the kingdom of God.
And another dieth in the bitterness of his soul, and never eateth with pleasure.
And changed his prison garments: and he did eat bread continually before him all the days of his life.
His flesh is consumed away, that it cannot be seen; and his bones that were not seen stick out.
In the body of his flesh through death, to present you holy and unblameable and unreproveable in his sight:
The flakes of his flesh are joined together: they are firm in themselves; they cannot be moved.
Which is his body, the fulness of him that filleth all in all.
Neither is there any creature that is not manifest in his sight: but all things are naked and opened unto the eyes of him with whom we have to do.
Yea, his soul draweth near unto the grave, and his life to the destroyers.
In Us grave they have laid him, he shunbers in peace. While his spirit In Paradise sweetly shell rest.
He keepeth back his soul from the pit, and his life from perishing by the sword.
He will deliver his soul from going into the pit, and his life shall see the light.
For my soul is full of troubles: and my life draweth nigh unto the grave.
If there be a messenger with him, an interpreter, one among a thousand, to shew unto man his uprightness:
But if there be no interpreter, let him keep silence in the church; and let him speak to himself, and to God.

And now, when Ammon saw that he was permitted to speak, he went forth and bowed himself before the king; and rising again he said: O king, I am very thankful before God this day that I am yet alive, and am permitted to speak; and I will endeavor to speak with boldness;
If he set his heart upon man, if he gather unto himself his spirit and his breath;
Then he is gracious unto him, and saith, Deliver him from going down to the pit: I have found a ransom.
He will not regard any ransom; neither will he rest content, though thou givest many gifts.
I have raised him up in righteousness, and I will direct all his ways: he shall build my city, and he shall let go my captives, not for price nor reward, saith the Lord of hosts.
If there be laid on him a sum of money, then he shall give for the ransom of his life whatsoever is laid upon him.

As soon as ye be come into the city, ye shall straightway find him, before he go up to the high place to eat: for the people will not eat until he come, because he doth bless the sacrifice; and afterwards they eat that be bidden. Now therefore get you up; for about this time ye shall find him.
His flesh shall be fresher than a child’s: he shall return to the days of his youth:
He that delicately bringeth up his servant from a child shall have him become his son at the length.
And he shall be unto thee a restorer of thy life, and a nourisher of thine old age: for thy daughter in law, which loveth thee, which is better to thee than seven sons, hath born him.
He shall pray unto God, and he will be favourable unto him: and he shall see his face with joy: for he will render unto man his righteousness.
He shall receive the blessing from the Lord, and righteousness from the God of his salvation.
He shall see the travail of his soul, and shall be satisfied; by his knowledge shall my righteous servant justify many; for he shall bear their iniquities.
And he shall judge the world in righteousness, he shall minister judgment to the people in uprightness.
The righteous also shall see, and fear, and shall laugh at him:
He shall see of the travail of his soul, and shall be satisfied: by his knowledge shall my righteous servant justify many; for he shall bear their iniquities.
He looketh upon men, and if any say, I have sinned, and perverted that which was right, and it profited me not;
Knowing that he that is such is subverted, and sinneth, being condemned of himself.

He said to me, “ Be simple and guileless, and 
you will be as the children who know not the 
wickedness that ruins the life of men. First, 
then, speak evil of no one, nor listen with pleas- 
ure to any one who speaks evil of another. But 
if you listen, you will partake of the sin of him
For he hath said, It profiteth a man nothing that he should delight himself with God.

1. He said to me: “Have simplicity and be 
innocent and you shall be as the children who do not 
know the wickedness that destroys the life of men. 
2. In the first place, speak evil of no one, and do 
not listen gladly to him who speaks evil. Other- 
wise you also by listening share in the sin of him 
who speaks evil, if you believe in the evil-speaking
He will deliver his soul from going into the pit, and his life shall see the light.
To bring back his soul from the pit, to be enlightened with the light of the living.
And shall consume the glory of his forest, and of his fruitful field, both soul and body; and they shall be as when a standard-bearer fainteth.
And shall consume the glory of his forest, and of his fruitful field, both soul and body: and they shall be as when a standardbearer fainteth.
Lo, all these things worketh God oftentimes with man,
Remember, remember that it is not the work of God that is frustrated, but the work of men;
But all these worketh that one and the selfsame Spirit, dividing to every man severally as he will.
And we know that all things work together for good to them that love God, to them who are the called according to his purpose.
There are many devices in a man’s heart; nevertheless the counsel of the Lord, that shall stand.
To bring back his soul from the pit, to be enlightened with the light of the living.
to overcome and be saved in His kingdom, which may He grant for Christ's sake. Amen.
To give light to them that sit in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide our feet into the way of peace.
To give knowledge of salvation unto his people by the remission of their sins,
Mark well, O Job, hearken unto me: hold thy peace, and I will speak.
Hold not thy peace, O God of my praise;
Hear, O my people, and I will testify unto thee: O Israel, if thou wilt hearken unto me;
Then shall ye call upon me, and ye shall go and pray unto me, and I will hearken unto you.
Wherefore, Job, I pray thee, hear my speeches, and hearken to all my words.
Verily I say unto you, even now, if he will hearken unto my voice, it shall be well with him. Even so. Amen.
If thou hast any thing to say, answer me: speak, for I desire to justify thee.
For by thy words thou shalt be justified, and by thy words thou shalt be condemned.
Keep thee far from a false matter; and the innocent and righteous slay thou not: for I will not justify the wicked.
Against thee, thee only, have I sinned, and done this evil in thy sight: that thou mightest be justified when thou speakest, and be clear when thou judgest.
Hear, I beseech thee, and I will speak: I will demand of thee, and declare thou unto me.
And now behold, I, the Lord, say unto you that ye are not justified, because these things are among you.
If not, hearken unto me: hold thy peace, and I shall teach thee wisdom.
And again, verily I say unto you, to some is given, by the Spirit of God, the word of wisdom.
I have taught thee in the way of wisdom; I have led thee in right paths.
O hearken, ye elders of my church, and give ear to the words which I shall speak unto you.
Hast thou heard the secret of God? and dost thou restrain wisdom to thyself?
Let men of understanding tell me, and let a wise man hearken unto me.
